session.user:
Search for and update remaining references to the old class names `GitHub` (the ABC class), `RealGitHub`, `FakeGitHub`, `DryRunGitHub`, `PrintingGitHub` in documentation files (.md) across the entire codebase at /home/runner/work/erk/erk.
IMPORTANT RULES: - Only update references that clearly refer to the ABC CLASS `GitHub` from `gateway/github/abc.py`, NOT the company/service "GitHub" - Look for patterns like `class GitHub`, `GitHub(ABC)`, `FakeGitHub(GitHub)`, code examples showing the class - The new names are: `LocalGitHub`, `RealLocalGitHub`, `FakeLocalGitHub`, `DryRunLocalGitHub`, `PrintingLocalGitHub` - Do NOT change references to: `GitHubIssues`, `GitHubAdmin`, `GitHubChecks`, `RemoteGitHub`, `GitHubRepoId`, etc. - Do NOT change references to "GitHub" when it means the company/website (e.g., "GitHub API", "GitHub Actions", "GitHub CLI") - Do NOT change variable names like `github`, `fake_github`, `ctx.github`
Search in these directories: - docs/learned/ - .claude/ - AGENTS.md - CLAUDE.md
The DryRun/Printing agents already updated some docs files. Focus on: 1. Code examples containing `class GitHub(ABC)`, `FakeGitHub(GitHub)`, `class RealGitHub(GitHub)` etc. 2. Text like "the GitHub ABC" → "the LocalGitHub ABC" in code-referencing contexts 3. Import examples like `from erk_shared.gateway.github.abc import GitHub`
Use grep to find all occurrences, read each file, and make targeted edits.

session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: FakeGitHub API Reference 3→read_when: 4→ - "writing tests that need fake GitHub PR or issue data" 5→ - "understanding FakeGitHub.create_pr() auto-registration" 6→ - "using mutation tracking in GitHub fake tests" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "creating a FakeGitHub PR without checking auto-registration in _pr_details" 9→ warning: "FakeGitHub.create_pr() auto-registers the PR in _pr_details. Manually adding to _pr_details after create_pr() causes duplicates." 10→ - action: "using context_for_test without matching parameter names to the current API" 11→ warning: "context_for_test() parameter names evolve. Check the current function signature before adding new parameters." 12→--- 13→ 14→# FakeGitHub API Reference 15→ 16→Reference for the FakeGitHub test double used throughout erk's test suite. Covers auto-registration behavior, mutation tracking, and common patterns. 17→ 18→## FakeGitHub.create_pr() Auto-Registration 19→ 20→When `FakeGitHub.create_pr()` is called, it automatically: 21→ 22→1. Creates the PR record 23→2. Registers it in `_pr_details` for subsequent `get_pr_details()` calls 24→3. Adds it to the open PRs list 25→ 26→Do NOT manually add to `_pr_details` after calling `create_pr()` — this causes duplicate entries. 27→ 28→## Mutation Tracking API 29→ 30→FakeGitHub tracks all mutations for test assertions:
... [59 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: GitHub Interface Patterns 3→last_audited: "2026-02-16 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: edited 5→read_when: 6→ - "calling GitHub API from erk" 7→ - "working with gh api command" 8→ - "fetching PR or issue data efficiently" 9→ - "understanding PRDetails type" 10→tripwires: 11→ - action: "adding a field to PullRequestInfo in types.py" 12→ warning: "Must update all three parsers in real.py: _parse_pr_from_timeline_event(), list_prs(), and _parse_plan_prs_with_details(). See PullRequestInfo Field Addition Protocol in this doc." 13→ score: 7 14→ - action: "adding a field to a GraphQL query that uses ISSUE_PR_LINKAGE_FRAGMENT" 15→ warning: "Check GET_PLAN_PRS_WITH_DETAILS_QUERY for divergence. Both queries fetch PR fields but are defined separately in graphql_queries.py. A field in one but not the other causes None values in some code paths." 16→ score: 5 17→--- 18→ 19→# GitHub Interface Patterns 20→ 21→This document describes patterns for efficient GitHub API access in the erk codebase. 22→ 23→## REST API via `gh api` 24→ 25→Prefer `gh api` for direct REST API access over `gh pr view --json` when you need comprehensive data in a single call. 26→ 27→### Why Use `gh api` 28→ 29→- **Single call efficiency**: Fetch all needed fields in one API request 30→- **Rate limit friendly**: Reduces number of API calls vs multiple `gh pr view --json` invocations
... [125 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→name: fake-driven-testing-gateway-architecture 3→description: Gateway layer architecture and interface patterns 4→--- 5→ 6→# Gateway Layer Architecture 7→ 8→**Read this when**: You need to understand or modify the gateway layer (the thin wrapper interfaces over external state). 9→ 10→## Overview 11→ 12→**Naming note**: "Gateway" is a common name for this pattern. These classes are also called **adapters**, **providers**, or **ports** in other contexts. The pattern matters more than the name. 13→ 14→## What Are Gateway Classes? 15→ 16→**Gateway classes are thin wrappers around heavyweight external APIs** that: 17→ 18→- Touch external state (filesystem, database, APIs, message queues) 19→- Could be slow (network calls, disk I/O, subprocess execution) 20→- Could fail periodically (network issues, rate limits, service outages) 21→- Are difficult to test directly 22→ 23→## The Four Implementations 24→ 25→Every gateway interface has **four implementations**: 26→ 27→### 1. Abstract Interface (ABC) 28→ 29→Defines the contract all implementations must follow. 30→
... [543 lines omitted] ...
113→ except Exception: 125→- Lets exceptions bubble up (no try/except for control flow) 158→ # Simulate failure if configured 160→ raise RuntimeError(f"Simulated failure for: {sql}") 344→ raise FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {path}") 363→ raise FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {path}") 494→ raise FileNotFoundError(f"File not found: {path}") 497→**Exception**: Fakes may create real directories when necessary for integration, but should not read/write actual files.

1→--- 2→title: Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist 3→last_audited: "2026-02-15 18:50 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "adding or modifying methods in any gateway ABC interface (Git, GitHub, Graphite)" 7→ - "implementing new gateway operations" 8→ - "composing one gateway inside another (e.g., GitHub composing GitHubIssues)" 9→tripwires: 10→ - action: "adding a new method to Git ABC" 11→ warning: "Must implement in 5 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py." 12→ - action: "adding a new method to GitHub ABC" 13→ warning: "Must implement in 5 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py." 14→ - action: "adding a new method to Graphite ABC" 15→ warning: "Must implement in 5 places: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py." 16→ - action: "removing an abstract method from a gateway ABC" 17→ warning: "Must remove from 5 places simultaneously: abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py. Partial removal causes type checker errors. Update all call sites to use subgateway property. Verify with grep across packages." 18→ - action: "adding subprocess.run or run_subprocess_with_context calls to a gateway real.py file" 19→ warning: "Must add integration tests in tests/integration/test_real_*.py. Real gateway methods with subprocess calls need tests that verify the actual subprocess behavior." 20→ pattern: "subprocess\\.run\\(|run_subprocess_with_context\\(" 21→ - action: "making N sequential gh api calls in a loop when a single GraphQL query could fetch all data" 22→ warning: "Each gh subprocess call costs ~200-300ms overhead. Batch into a single GraphQL query with node fragments when fetching multiple items. See http-accelerated-plan-refresh.md for the dual-path pattern." 23→ - action: "using subprocess.run with git command outside of a gateway" 24→ warning: "Use the Git gateway instead. Direct subprocess calls bypass testability (fakes) and dry-run support. The Git ABC (erk_shared.gateway.git.abc.Git) likely already has a method for this operation. Only use subprocess directly in real.py gateway implementations." 25→ pattern: "subprocess\\.run\\(\\s*\\[.*[\"']git" 26→ - action: "changing gateway return type to discriminated union" 27→ warning: "Verify all 5 implementations import the new types. Missing imports in abc.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, or printing.py break the gateway pattern." 28→ - action: "designing error handling for a new gateway method" 29→ warning: "Ask: does the caller continue after the failure? If yes, use discriminated union. If all callers terminate, use exceptions. See 'Non-Ideal State Decision Checklist' section." 30→ - action: "adding a new parameter to a gateway ABC method"
... [772 lines omitted] ...
31→ warning: "All 5 implementations must be updated (ABC, Real, Fake, DryRun, Printing). Fake may accept but not track new parameters when assertion is not needed for tests." 42→**Exception: erk-dev** (`packages/erk-dev/`) is developer tooling and is exempt from these rules. Direct `subprocess.run` with git commands is acceptable in erk-dev since it doesn't need gateway abstractions for its own operations. 136→When designing error handling for a new gateway method, the key question is: **does the caller continue after the failure?** 138→This checklist helps you choose between discriminated unions and exceptions. For full pattern documentation, see [Discriminated Union Error Handling](discriminated-union-error-handling.md). 140→### Decision: Discriminated Union or Exception? 144→| Does at least one caller branch on the error and continue with different logic? | Discriminated union | Exception | 145→| Does the error carry domain-meaningful fields beyond just `message`? | Discriminated union | Exception | 146→| Do all callers terminate on failure (extract message and stop)? | Exception | Discriminated union | 150→- [ ] At least one caller branches on the error and continues with different logic 151→- [ ] Error type carries domain-meaningful fields beyond just `message` 152→- [ ] Error type implements the `NonIdealState` protocol (`error_type` + `message` properties) from `erk_shared.non_ideal_state` 156→### Checklist: Use Exceptions When 158→- [ ] All callers terminate on failure (extract message and stop) 159→- [ ] No caller inspects error type or branches on error content 160→- [ ] Use `UserFacingCliError` at CLI boundary 166→All discriminated union error types must implement the `NonIdealState` protocol from `packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/non_ideal_state.py`. The protocol requires two read-only properties: `error_type` (machine-readable classification string) and `message` (human-readable description). 181→**Methods using discriminated unions** (callers branch on error): 185→| `merge_pr` | `MergeResult \| MergeError` | GitHub | 186→| `push_to_remote` | `PushResult \| PushError` | Git remote_ops | 187→| `pull_rebase` | `PullRebaseResult \| PullRebaseError` | Git remote_ops | 189→| `submit_branch` | `SubmitBranchResult \| SubmitBranchError` | BranchManager | 191→**Methods using exceptions** (all callers terminate): 193→| Method | Exception | Why | 195→| Worktree add/remove | `RuntimeError` | No caller inspects error content; all terminate identically | 196→| HTTP operations | `HttpError` | Generic transport failure; callers just surface the message | 200→When changing an existing gateway method's return type (e.g., converting from exception-based to discriminated union), follow this comprehensive migration pattern: 207→ - [ ] `real.py` - Return appropriate error types for failure cases 209→ - [ ] `dry_run.py` - Return appropriate success/error based on mode 212→5. [ ] Update tests to check `isinstance(result, ErrorType)` 217→**PR #6294** (`merge_pr: bool | str` → `MergeResult | MergeError`): 221→- Updated tests to use `isinstance(result, MergeError)` 243→Some resources benefit from existence-check methods that enable Look Before You Leap validation. This pattern prevents cryptic errors when fetching non-existent resources. 251→- When error messages from `get_X()` on missing resources are unclear 274→ user_output(f"Error: Issue #{issue_number} not found") 353→5. **Unit tests** (`tests/unit/`) - Update any tests that assumed failure on missing resource 597→The worktree sub-gateway follows the same 5-file pattern with methods: `list_worktrees()`, `add_worktree()`, `move_worktree()`, `remove_worktree()`, `prune_worktrees()`, `find_worktree_for_branch()`. Note: Worktree operations use exceptions (RuntimeError), not discriminated unions. 686→**Partial removal causes type checker errors** - if you remove from abc.py but forget printing.py, the type checker will complain that PrintingGit doesn't implement the abstract method. 704→1. Run type checker (`ty`) to verify no abstract method errors 761→├── abc.py # push_to_remote() -> PushResult | PushError 763→├── fake.py # Constructor-injected errors, mutation tracking 770→- **Discriminated union returns**: Methods return `Success | Error` instead of raising 771→- **Error boundary in real.py**: `try/except RuntimeError` converts to `PushError` 772→- **Fake configuration**: `FakeGitRemoteOps(push_to_remote_error=PushError(...))` injects failures 795→- [Gateway Error Boundaries](gateway-error-boundaries.md) - Where exceptions become discriminated unions
session.assistant: Now let me read a few more key files to understand the scope better:
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→title: FakeGitHub Mutation Tracking 3→read_when: 4→ - "asserting against FakeGitHub mutations in tests" 5→ - "adding a new mutation tracking list to FakeGitHub or FakeGitHubIssues" 6→ - "understanding tuple formats in fake gateway tracking lists" 7→tripwires: 8→ - action: "adding a tracking list without documenting the tuple field order" 9→ warning: "Every tracking list must have a property docstring specifying the tuple format (e.g., 'Returns list of (pr_number, label) tuples'). Without it, test authors guess field positions wrong." 10→--- 11→ 12→# FakeGitHub Mutation Tracking 13→ 14→The `FakeGitHub` and `FakeGitHubIssues` classes track all mutations as lists of tuples. Tests assert against these lists to verify correct API call sequences without hitting real GitHub. 15→ 16→## Tuple Format Convention 17→ 18→<!-- Source: packages/erk-shared/src/erk_shared/gateway/github/fake.py:145-171 --> 19→ 20→Every tracking list uses a typed tuple with a specific field order. The convention is `list[tuple[field1_type, field2_type, ...]]` with the field order documented in the property docstring. 21→ 22→## FakeGitHub Tracking Lists 23→ 24→| List | Tuple Format | Example | 25→| -------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ | 26→| `_downloaded_artifacts` | `(run_id, artifact_name, destination)` | `("123", "results", Path("/tmp/out"))` | 27→| `_created_gists` | `(filename, content, description, public)` | `("log.txt", "data", "desc", False)` | 28→| `_updated_pr_bases` | `(pr_number, new_base)` | `(42, "main")` | 29→| `_updated_pr_bodies` | `(pr_number, body)` | `(42, "new body")` | 30→| `_updated_pr_titles` | `(pr_number, title)` | `(42, "new title")` |
... [64 lines omitted] ...

1→--- 2→title: Code Conventions 3→read_when: 4→ - "naming functions or variables" 5→ - "creating CLI commands" 6→ - "naming Claude artifacts" 7→ - "moving code between packages" 8→ - "creating imports" 9→ - "creating immutable classes or frozen dataclasses" 10→ - "implementing an ABC with abstract properties" 11→tripwires: 12→ - action: "writing `__all__` to a Python file" 13→ warning: "Re-export modules are forbidden. Import directly from where code is defined." 14→ - action: "adding --force flag to a CLI command" 15→ warning: 'Always include -f as the short form. Pattern: @click.option("-f", "--force", ...)' 16→ - action: "adding a function with 5+ parameters" 17→ warning: "Load `dignified-python` skill first. Use keyword-only arguments (add `*` after first param). Exception: ABC/Protocol method signatures and Click command callbacks." 18→ - action: "editing source files directly on master branch" 19→ warning: "Never edit source files on master. Even for one-line fixes, use plan-first workflow. Bypasses review, CI gates, and worktree isolation." 20→last_audited: "2026-02-16 14:20 PT" 21→audit_result: clean 22→--- 23→ 24→# Code Conventions 25→ 26→This document defines conventions that supplement AGENTS.md. For basic naming (snake_case, PascalCase, kebab-case), Claude artifacts naming, brand names, worktree terminology, and CLI command organization, see AGENTS.md directly. 27→ 28→## Variable Naming by Type 29→ 30→| Type | Convention | Example |
... [212 lines omitted] ...
145→class GitHubAPIFailed: 154→class GitHubAPIFailed: 220→## Pydantic Field() Exception 222→Pydantic `Field()` with defaults is an exception to erk's "no default parameters" rule. This applies only to Pydantic config/settings classes (e.g., `BaseSettings` subclasses), NOT to regular functions or dataclasses. 224→## Single-Use Locals Line-Length Exception

1→--- 2→title: Erk Glossary 3→last_audited: "2026-02-16 00:00 PT" 4→audit_result: clean 5→read_when: 6→ - "understanding project terminology" 7→ - "confused about domain-specific terms" 8→ - "working with worktrees, plans, or stacks" 9→ - "working with objectives or turns" 10→tripwires: 11→ - action: "parsing objective roadmap PR column status" 12→ warning: "Roadmap table uses separate Plan and PR columns: Plan `#XXXX`=in progress (plan issue), PR `#XXXX`=done (merged PR), both empty=pending. Legacy 4-col format with `plan #XXXX` in PR column is auto-migrated." 13→--- 14→ 15→# Erk Glossary 16→ 17→Definitive terminology reference for the erk project. 18→ 19→**Purpose**: Eliminate confusion about domain-specific terms. When in doubt about terminology, consult this document. 20→ 21→--- 22→ 23→## Core Concepts 24→ 25→### activate.sh 26→ 27→A shell script at `.erk/activate.sh` in each worktree that sets up the development environment when sourced. 28→ 29→**Purpose**: Opt-in shell integration. Users explicitly source this script rather than relying on automatic shell manipulation. 30→
... [1038 lines omitted] ...
543→A sentinel class returned when a PR lookup fails, preserving lookup context (branch name, PR number). Uses `isinstance` check (LBYL) instead of exceptions. 585→- `style: Literal["info", "success", "warning", "error"]` - Visual style hint (default: "info") 737→- `tripwires`: List of action-triggered warnings 748→ warning: "Use .erk/scratch/<session-id>/ instead." 775→**Format**: Defined in doc frontmatter with `action` (pattern to detect) and `warning` (guidance message). 784→ warning: "AI workflow files belong in .erk/scratch/<session-id>/, NOT /tmp/." 862→- `erk land` skips the "not learned from" warning for learn plans (they don't need learning) 968→- **EAFP**: Easier to Ask for Forgiveness than Permission (exception-based error handling) 969→- **LBYL**: Look Before You Leap (check-before-operation error handling) 1033→**Purpose**: Break large, complex goals into reviewable, implementable chunks. Instead of tackling "migrate all errors to Ensure class" in one massive PR, an objective evaluates the codebase, identifies a manageable subset of violations, and creates a plan for that subset.

# Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist
All gateway ABCs (Git, GitHub, Graphite) follow the same 5-file pattern. When adding a new method to any gateway, you must implement it in **5 places**:

# Gateway ABC Implementation Checklist
All gateway ABCs (Git, LocalGitHub, Graphite) follow the same 5-file pattern. When adding a new method to any gateway, you must implement it in **5 places**:

## FakeGateway Pattern for Mutations
When adding a mutation method to a Fake:
```python ...[truncated 664 chars]...def thread_replies(self) -> list[tuple[str, str]]: return self._thread_replies ```

## FakeGateway Pattern for Mutations
When adding a mutation method to a Fake:
```python ...[truncated 674 chars]...def thread_replies(self) -> list[tuple[str, str]]: return self._thread_replies ```

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
## Common Pitfall
**Printing implementations often fall behind** - when adding a new method, verify PrintingGit/PrintingLocalGitHub/PrintingGraphite is updated alongside the other implementations.

## Common Pitfall
**Printing implementations often fall behind** - when adding a new method, verify PrintingGit/PrintingLocalGitHub/PrintingGraphite is updated alongside the other implementations.

session.tool_result: <tool_use_error>No changes to make: old_string and new_string are exactly the same.</tool_use_error>

session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→name: fake-driven-testing-gateway-architecture 3→description: Gateway layer architecture and interface patterns 4→--- 5→ 6→# Gateway Layer Architecture 7→ 8→**Read this when**: You need to understand or modify the gateway layer (the thin wrapper interfaces over external state). 9→ 10→## Overview 11→ 12→**Naming note**: "Gateway" is a common name for this pattern. These classes are also called **adapters**, **providers**, or **ports** in other contexts. The pattern matters more than the name. 13→ 14→## What Are Gateway Classes? 15→ 16→**Gateway classes are thin wrappers around heavyweight external APIs** that: 17→ 18→- Touch external state (filesystem, database, APIs, message queues) 19→- Could be slow (network calls, disk I/O, subprocess execution) 20→- Could fail periodically (network issues, rate limits, service outages) 21→- Are difficult to test directly 22→ 23→## The Four Implementations 24→ 25→Every gateway interface has **four implementations**: 26→ 27→### 1. Abstract Interface (ABC) 28→ 29→Defines the contract all implementations must follow. 30→
... [75 lines omitted] ...

.claude/skills/fake-driven-testing/references/gateway-architecture.md:511:| **Testing** | Use `FakeGitHub` directly | Use `PlannedPRBackend(FakeGitHub(), FakeGitHubIssues(), time=FakeTime())` | .claude/skills/fake-driven-testing/references/gateway-architecture.md:542: fake_github = FakeGitHub() .claude/skills/dignified-python/references/api-design.md:61:[Omitted long matching line] .claude/skills/dignified-python/references/api-design.md:176:class FakeGitHub: .claude/skills/dignified-python/references/api-design.md:185:class FakeGitHub: .claude/skills/refac-cli-push-down/SKILL.md:69: def test_check_pr_status_returns_open_prs(fake_github: FakeGitHub) -> None: .claude/skills/objective/references/format.md:253:| 3.1 | Create FakeGitHub with just `create_pr()` | pending | | .claude/skills/objective/references/format.md:256:**Test:** PR creation test uses FakeGitHub. .claude/skills/objective/references/format.md:262:| 4.1 | Add remaining FakeGitHub methods | pending | | .claude/skills/objective/references/format.md:265:**Test:** All GitHub gateway tests use FakeGitHub. .claude/skills/objective/references/format.md:281:- FakeGitHub must implement all methods from GitHub ABC .claude/skills/objective/references/format.md:286:- Tests should use FakeGitHub instead of subprocess mocking .claude/skills/objective/references/format.md:417:- Started work on FakeGitHub .claude/skills/objective/references/format.md:428: - 2.1a: FakeGitHub core (pending) .claude/skills/objective/references/format.md:429: - 2.1b: FakeGitHub authentication (pending)

session.assistant: Let me update these skill files:
session.tool_result:
505→| Aspect | Gateway | Backend | 506→| ------------------- | -------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | 507→| **Purpose** | Thin wrapper around external system | Higher-level abstraction that composes gateways | 508→| **Examples** | `GitHub`, `Git`, `Graphite`, `Shell`, `Time` | `PlannedPRBackend`, `PlanBackend` implementations | 509→| **Implementations** | 4: ABC, Real, Fake, DryRun | Just ABC + real implementations | 510→| **Needs Fake?** | ✅ Yes - provides in-memory simulation | ❌ No - inject fake gateways instead | 511→| **Testing** | Use `FakeGitHub` directly | Use `PlannedPRBackend(FakeGitHub(), FakeGitHubIssues(), time=FakeTime())` | 512→ 513→### Backend Architecture 514→ 515→Backends are higher-level abstractions that: 516→ 517→1. **Compose one or more gateways** via constructor injection 518→2. **Transform data** from gateway-specific to domain models 519→3. **Implement domain operations** (not raw external operations) 520→ 521→```python 522→# Backend takes gateways as constructor arguments 523→class PlannedPRBackend(PlanBackend): 524→ def __init__(self, github: GitHub, github_issues: GitHubIssues, *, time: Time): 525→ self._github = github # Injects gateway 526→ self._github_issues = github_issues 527→ self._time = time 528→ 529→ def create_plan(self, ...) -> CreatePlanResult: 530→ # Uses gateway to implement domain operation 531→ result = self._github.create_pr(...) 532→ return CreatePlanResult(plan_id=str(result.number), url=result.url) 533→``` 534→
... [25 lines omitted] ...

session.assistant: Now let me update the dignified-python skill file:
session.tool_result:
170→**Don't add parameters to fakes "just in case" they might be useful for testing.** 171→ 172→Fakes should mirror production interfaces. Adding test-only configuration knobs that never get used creates dead code and false complexity. 173→ 174→```python 175→# WRONG: Test-only parameter that's never used in production 176→class FakeGitHub: 177→ def __init__( 178→ self, 179→ prs: dict[str, PullRequestInfo] | None = None, 180→ rate_limited: bool = False, # "Might test this later" 181→ ) -> None: 182→ self._rate_limited = rate_limited # Never set to True anywhere 183→ 184→# CORRECT: Only add infrastructure when you need it 185→class FakeGitHub: 186→ def __init__( 187→ self, 188→ prs: dict[str, PullRequestInfo] | None = None, 189→ ) -> None: 190→ ... 191→``` 192→ 193→**The test for this:** If grep shows a parameter is only ever passed in test files, and those tests are testing hypothetical scenarios rather than actual production behavior, delete both the parameter and the tests. 194→ 195→--- 196→ 197→## Speculative Tests 198→ 199→```python
... [5 lines omitted] ...
